diabetes.name: Brisk Walking
diabetes.description: 30-minute brisk walk significantly lowers blood sugar by increasing insulin sensitivity in muscle cells.
steps: Start at a slow pace for 5 min warm-up
Increase to brisk pace — you should be able to talk but not sing
Cool down with slow walk for 5 min
diabetes.caution: Check blood sugar before exercise. Carry glucose tablets. Avoid if blood sugar > 300 mg/dL.

diabetes.name: Yoga – Surya Namaskar
diabetes.description: 12 postures in sequence improve circulation, reduce cortisol, and help stabilize blood sugar.
diabetes.caution: Start with 4 rounds and build up. Avoid if you have back injury.

diabetes.name: Resistance Band Exercises
diabetes.description: Resistance training builds muscle mass which acts as a glucose sink, improving long-term blood sugar control.
diabetes.caution: Monitor blood sugar before and after. Eat a light snack if blood sugar < 120 mg/dL before exercise.

hypertension.name: Deep Breathing – Pranayama
hypertension.description: Slow deep breathing activates the parasympathetic nervous system, lowering heart rate and blood pressure.
steps: Practice Anulom Vilom (alternate nostril) for 5 min
hypertension.caution: Never strain while breathing. If dizzy, breathe normally.

hypertension.name: Swimming / Water Walking
hypertension.description: Water exercise is gentle on joints while providing excellent cardiovascular benefit and BP reduction.
steps: Warm up with water walking for 5 min
Swim freestyle or breaststroke at comfortable pace
hypertension.caution: Do not exercise if BP > 180/110 mmHg. Consult doctor before starting.

pcod.name: HIIT – High Intensity Interval Training
pcod.description: Short bursts of intense exercise followed by rest periods improve insulin sensitivity and help regulate hormones in PCOD.
steps: Warm up 5 min with light jogging
pcod.caution: Not recommended if very overweight — start with low impact. Stop if you feel pain or extreme fatigue.

pcod.name: Yoga – PCOD Sequence
pcod.description: Specific yoga poses stimulate the ovaries, balance hormones, and reduce cortisol which disrupts menstrual cycles.
steps: Legs Up the Wall (Viparita Karani) – 5 min
pcod.caution: Avoid inversions during periods. Practice on empty stomach.

stress.name: Meditation – Body Scan
stress.description: Systematic body awareness practice that activates the relaxation response and lowers cortisol significantly.
steps: Close eyes and take 3 deep breaths
Focus on your feet — notice any sensations
Slowly move awareness upward: legs, hips, abdomen, chest
Continue to arms, shoulders, neck, face
When mind wanders, gently bring it back
End with 3 deep breaths and open eyes slowly
stress.caution: It's normal for the mind to wander — this is not failure. Simply return attention.

stress.name: Evening Walk in Nature
stress.description: Walking in green spaces (parks, gardens) has been proven to lower cortisol and improve mood better than urban walks.
steps: Leave phone on silent if possible
No destination needed — enjoy the walk itself

back_pain.name: Cat-Cow Stretch
back_pain.description: Gentle spinal flexion and extension lubricates vertebral joints and relieves tension in back muscles.
steps: Get on hands and knees (tabletop position)
Inhale — drop belly toward floor, lift head and tailbone (Cow)
Exhale — round spine toward ceiling, tuck chin and pelvis (Cat)
back_pain.caution: Stop if movement causes sharp or shooting pain.

back_pain.name: Child's Pose (Balasana)
back_pain.description: Gentle resting pose that lengthens the lower back and releases tension in the hips and spine.
steps: Kneel on floor, sit back on heels
Fold forward, extending arms overhead or alongside body
Breathe deeply into the back body
back_pain.caution: Avoid if you have knee injury. Place a pillow between thighs and calves if needed.

obesity.name: Low-Impact Aerobics
obesity.description: Low-impact aerobic exercise burns calories while being gentle on joints, making it ideal for people who are overweight.
steps: Side steps with arm swings 3 min
obesity.caution: Start with 20 min and gradually increase. Stay hydrated.

thyroid.name: Sarvangasana (Shoulder Stand)
thyroid.description: Inverted pose that increases blood flow to the thyroid gland, potentially stimulating underactive thyroid function.
steps: Lift torso until body is vertical
thyroid.caution: Avoid with high BP, glaucoma, neck injury, or during menstruation. Learn from instructor first.

general_fitness.name: Bodyweight Workout
general_fitness.description: Full-body workout using no equipment, builds strength and stamina.
general_fitness.caution: Maintain proper form over quantity. Stop if you feel joint pain.
